decisions.title: Standardize LegendV2 Implementation Across All Chart Types
decisions.context: Three parallel Legend V2 efforts (Scatter, GeoMap, Histogram) suggest we need a unified approach to avoid code duplication and ensure consistent UX. This is core 'Nail the Core' work for Explore V2.

arguments.for: Reduces tech debt, ensures consistent user experience across all chart types, accelerates future legend features. Single implementation means less maintenance burden.
arguments.against: May require refactoring existing work, could delay individual chart releases. Each chart type has unique legend requirements that might not fit a single abstraction.

decisions.title: Prioritize Python Exec vs CEL for Rule Expression Language
decisions.context: Simultaneous CEL syntax highlighting work (ENG-10519) and Python Exec Alarms (ENG-10454) suggest we're supporting two expression languages. Need clarity on which is the strategic bet for 'Python Rules' product.

arguments.for: Python aligns with data science workflows and our 'Python Rules' product positioning. CEL is lighter-weight and sandboxed. Clarifying strategy prevents wasted engineering effort.
arguments.against: Both may serve different use cases (CEL for simple rules, Python for complex). Maintaining both increases surface area but provides flexibility.

decisions.title: Commit to Ask SIFT Conversation State Machine Architecture
decisions.context: ENG-10385 is building out conversation state machine and ENG-10624 enables chat archiving. This is foundational for 'Ask SIFT' product becoming a decision-capture tool vs just Q&A. Relates to 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' North Star.

arguments.for: State machine enables multi-turn reasoning, context retention, and decision workflow orchestration. Critical for moving beyond simple query-response to actual decision support.
arguments.against: Adds complexity. Simple stateless Q&A may be sufficient for v1. State management introduces debugging and testing challenges.

decisions.title: Resolve FedRAMP Branch Sync Strategy
decisions.context: ENG-10702 syncing FedRAMP branch to main suggests ongoing divergence. This creates maintenance burden and delays feature parity for government customers. Relates to 'Build the Moat' via compliance positioning.
decisions.blockedBy: Need compliance review on which features can/cannot be merged. Infrastructure differences between FedRAMP and standard deployments.

arguments.for: Single codebase reduces maintenance burden, accelerates feature delivery to FedRAMP customers, reduces drift and merge conflicts.
arguments.against: FedRAMP has unique security/compliance requirements that may require separate code paths. Feature flags could become unwieldy.

decisions.title: Archive or Accelerate Sift Edge GUI Development
decisions.context: ENG-10561 improving Edge connect logic suggests ongoing investment, but limited other Edge signals. Need to decide if this is strategic 'Embed Deeper' pillar work or should be deprioritized.

arguments.for: Edge deployments enable embedded use cases and offline operation - key differentiator. GUI improvements make Edge accessible to non-engineers, expanding TAM.
arguments.against: Limited adoption signals. Resources might be better spent on core Explore V2 or Canvas. Edge may be solution looking for a problem.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy engineering execution week with 30 active tickets spanning 6 product areas. Fragmentation risk: work distributed across Explore V2 polish (legends, zoom, families), Ask SIFT infrastructure, Edge improvements, compliance, and documentation. Limited signals on Canvas or strategic integration work.
alerts: Three separate Legend V2 implementations (Scatter, GeoMap, Histogram) - coordination needed
Only 1 UXR study signal (DES-156/158) - insufficient user validation for Q2 releases
FedRAMP branch divergence at 89 commits - technical debt accumulating
No Canvas-related engineering tickets - at risk of de-prioritization despite being strategic pillar
Multiple family-related bugs (ENG-8213, ENG-10597) suggest core feature still not stable
